title: Kernel bootstrap for this Codex session. Read and execute the repository-authoritative prompt at: /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts/kernel.md Run id: adhoc:MacBook-Pro-M5-5:kernel-workspace:42297:run:20260402T093918:54970 Bootstrap receipt path: /Users/masayuki/.config/ker...
summary_text: Repo-side fix landed in [install-kernel-launchers.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh#L141) and [tests/test-install-kernel-launchers.sh](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-install-kernel-launchers.sh#L164). The installer now provisions `~/.codex/prompts/{kernel,k,vote,v}.md` before helper-launcher preflight, and helper resolution no longer dies silently inside command substitution. In a clean shell missing `codex-prompt-launch`, the prompt symlinks still get installed, then the installer fails explicitly with a clear helper error instead of skipping slash-prompt installation entirely. Verified: - `bash tests/test-install-kernel-launchers.sh` - `bash tests/test-kernel-launcher.sh` - `bash tests/test-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` One important limit remains: `RUN_CODEX_KERNEL_SMOKE=1 bash tests/test-codex-kernel-prompt.sh` still fails in this environment because `codex exec /kernel` does not emit the repo’s required Kernel bootstrap acknowledgement. That matches the repo contract that bare slash handling is still upstream/session-dependent, not fully repo-controlled. So the repo-side regression is fixed, but this current chat may still need a fresh repo-root Codex session to pick up `/kernel` and `/k`; if the slash parser is still stale, use `kernel` as the local fallback.
